title: Sweet Indulgent British Pudding Spotted-Dick
description: A traditional Northern-Irish dessert featuring a moist sponge cake, covered in a rich fruit suet pudding mixture, and served warm. Perfect for a comforting treat or special occasion.
instructions: Grease a 1-pound (450g) ceramic tagine or large mixing bowl and set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and salt. Add the softened butter and mix until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, suet, milk, and vanilla extract.
Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Do not overmix.
Pour in the mixed fruit and stir until evenly distributed.
Pour the mixture into the prepared tagine or bowl and cover with a lid or plastic wrap.
Bake for 45-50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with a dollop of whipped cream or a sprinkle of sugar if desired.
